_doc: Per-city cost evidence. One file per city: data/cost-evidence/<slug>.json (gitignored). This file (_schema.json) and _example.json are tracked so the structure lives in git without committing the raw research. scripts/build_costs.py reads every file here and writes rent/util/var/solo/couple + monthly cost1/cost2 into data/travel-data.json. The number in git is therefore a function of sourced evidence kept here.
_anchor: Every estimate is anchored to ONE persona: a solo nomad living mid-range — furnished 1BR in a nomad-popular neighborhood, cooks some + eats out some, uses a coworking space. Party size scales up from this via each component's `party` rule.
city: Exact city name as it appears in travel-data.json (used to match). Required.
provenance: legacy-seed | researched. legacy-seed = migrated from the old hand-seeded numbers (low confidence, carries monthlyOverride so the build is byte-identical to before). researched = real sourced components; builder computes the monthly numbers.

components._doc: Monthly USD for the solo anchor. solo = sum of all .usd. Each component: usd, party, source, asOf, confidence (low|med|high), note.

_partyRules: shared = +15% for a second person (×1.15). perPerson = ×1.90 (a couple shares some but not all). perPersonDiscounted = ×1.60. Tunable as PARTY_MULT in scripts/build_costs.py. couple = sum of each component scaled by its party rule.

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 multipliers (Jan..Dec) applied to RENT ONLY (food/transit don't swing with tourist season). Default all 1.0. Used only when monthlyOverride is absent.

monthlyOverride._doc: OPTIONAL. If present, builder uses these 12-length arrays verbatim and ignores components/seasonality for the monthly numbers (solo/couple still come from components). Legacy-seed files carry this so migration is behavior-preserving. Delete it once a city is genuinely researched, and the builder will compute from components instead.

evidence._doc: Free-form qualitative receipts — the bespoke part. Reddit/forum quotes, blog figures, Numbeo snapshots. Not read by the builder; this is the audit trail behind the component numbers.
evidence.claim: what this supports, e.g. '1BR furnished Nimman ~12-15k THB/mo'
